description: conversing about Alex and Priya are discussing the best vector database for their production RAG system, comparing Pinecone vs Weaviate vs pgvector, and deciding to benchmark all three this week
embedding_key: conversing about Alex and Priya are discussing the best vector database for their production RAG system, comparing Pinecone vs Weaviate vs pgvector, and deciding to benchmark all three this week
filling: Alex Chen
Hey Priya, I've been looking into vector databases for our RAG pipeline. Have you had any experience with Pinecone?
Priya Sharma
Yeah, Pinecone is solid but pricey at scale. Have you considered Weaviate or even pgvector since we already use Postgres?
Alex Chen
Good point about pgvector. Let's benchmark all three this week and compare latency and recall metrics.
Priya Sharma
Sounds like a plan. I'll set up the Weaviate instance and you handle Pinecone. We can share results Friday.

Alex Chen

subject: Alex Chen
description: conversing about Alex and Priya are discussing the hallucination problem in the chatbot and Priya is suggesting using retrieval-augmented generation with strict source attribution to reduce fabricated answers
embedding_key: conversing about Alex and Priya are discussing the hallucination problem in the chatbot and Priya is suggesting using retrieval-augmented generation with strict source attribution to reduce fabricated answers
filling: Alex Chen
Priya, the chatbot is still hallucinating product specs. We need a better solution than just prompt engineering.
Priya Sharma
I think we should enforce strict source attribution. Every claim in the response must map back to a retrieved document chunk.
Alex Chen
That's a great idea. We could add a verification step that checks each fact against the source chunks.
Priya Sharma
Exactly. And if the model can't ground a claim, it should say it doesn't know rather than making something up.
